revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Kujō|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原任子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Kujō Ninshi
{{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Toba]]. She was the daughter of [[Sessho|regent]] [[Kujō Kanezane]]. Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) |Shōshi]]'' (昇子内親王) (1195–1211) - unmarried Empress as adopted mother of Emperor Juntoku (Shunkamon-in, 春華門院) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Sukeko]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Noriko (1177–1210)|Princess Noriko]] | years=1190–1200}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kujo, Ninshi}} [[Category:1173 births]] [[Category:1239 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhists]] [[Category:Buddhism and women]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Toba]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhists]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[en:Kujō Ninshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[Category:Pure Land Buddhists]] [[en:Kujō Ninshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[en:Kujō Ninshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] {{Japan-royal-stub}}

{{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. {{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Toba]]. She was the daughter of [[Sessho|regent]] [[Kujō Kanezane]]. Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) |Shōshi]]'' (昇子内親王) (1195–1211) - unmarried Empress as adopted mother of Emperor Juntoku (Shunkamon-in, 春華門院) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Sukeko]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Noriko (1177–1210)|Princess Noriko]] | years=1190–1200}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kujo, {{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. {{family name hatnote|Kujō|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原任子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Kujō Ninshi
{{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Toba]]. She was the daughter of [[Sessho|regent]] [[Kujō Kanezane]]. Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) |Shōshi]]'' (昇子内親王) (1195–1211) - unmarried Empress as adopted mother of Emperor Juntoku (Shunkamon-in, 春華門院) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess {{family name hatnote|Kujō|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原任子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Kujō Ninshi
{{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[empress consort of Japan]]. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Toba]]. She was the daughter of [[Sessho|regent]] [[Kujō Kanezane]]. Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) |Shōshi]]'' (昇子内親王) (1195–1211) - unmarried Empress as adopted mother of Emperor Juntoku (Shunkamon-in, 春華門院) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Sukeko]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Noriko (1177–1210)|Princess Noriko]] | years=1190–1200}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1173 births]] [[Category:1239 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhists]] [[Category:Buddhism and women]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Toba]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhists]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pure Land Buddhists]] }} [[sn:Kujō Ninshi]] [[en:Kujō Ninshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:کوجو نینشی]] [[fr:Kujō Ninshi]] [[id:Kujō Ninshi]] [[ja:九条任子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Ninshi]] [[th:คูโจ นินชิ]] [[zh:九条任子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{lang|ja|九条任子}} |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 31 May 1190 – 9 August 1200 |consort = yes |spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]]|1190}} |issue = [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1195–1211)|Princess Shōshi]] |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] |mother = Fujiwara no Tomoko |birth_date = 31 October 1173 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death date and age|1239|2|3|1173|10|31|df=yes}} |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |burial_date = |burial_place = |}} {{nihongo|'''Kujō Ninshi'''|九条 任子||31 October 1173 – 3 February 1239}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Ninshi'''|藤原 任子}} was an [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Sessho|regent]] [[:en:Kujō Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]]. Upon the death of her mother in 1201, she [[:en:Buddhist initiation ritual|undertook the Buddhist precepts]] under the monk [[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]. ;Issue: * Imperial Princess ''[[:en:Princess Shōshi (1195–1211) |Shōshi]]'' (昇子内親王) (1195–1211) - unmarried Empress as adopted mother of Emperor Juntoku (Shunkamon-in, 春華門院) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Sukeko|Princess Sukeko]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Noriko (1177–1210)|Princess Noriko]] | years=1190–1200}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] 2]] {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Kujō Ninshi]] [[fa:کوجو نینشی]] [[fr:Kujō Ninshi]] [[id:Kujō Ninshi]] [[ja:九条任子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Ninshi]] [[th:คูโจ นินชิ]] [[zh:九条任子]] {{wikidata link|Q6442369}} [[Category:1239 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhists]] [[Category:Buddhism and women]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Toba]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhists]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pure Land Buddhists]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
